RO: Danube delta wetland maze; forested eastern ridges above wide interior subsidence basins
RS: Pannonian flats with braided channels; limestone gorges in eastern fold-thrust ranges
RU: Taiga on ancient shields; meandering lowland rivers under northern permafrost tables
RW: Ridge country between rift lake basins; afroalpine moor on wind-exposed summits
SA: Great escarpment cliffs over gravel sheets; coral-fringed dry coast below coastal rims
SB: Volcanic peaks above ocean trenches; humid windward cloud forest around reef passages
SC: Granitic peaks over humid canopy; outer atolls on a wide submarine bank margin
SD: Braided clay plains at major river joins; eastward lateritic uplands in acacia savanna
SE: Scoured shields and skerry mazes; brackish inner seas on smoothed glacial bedrock coasts
SG: Reclaimed terraces and sand grids; mangrove harbor margins on shallow tropical shelves
SI: Julian limestone peaks; hanging valleys and lakes between narrow fault-block ranges
SK: High granite walls with cirque tarns; wide low plains threaded by braided river meanders
SL: Forested peaks over mangrove estuaries; lateritic scarps down to broad coastal mudflats
SM: Three limestone crests over patchwork plains; steep cliffs above small alluvial fans below
SN: Sahel Atlantic margin; inland acacia parkland grading to eastward semi-desert gravels
SO: Arid Indian Ocean littoral; dissected gneissic plateaus with thorn scrub on pale regolith
SR: Interior rainforested shield; coastal mangrove maze at muddy Atlantic river mouths
SS: Seasonal swamp grass on clay; flooded tropical flats between woodland savanna belts
ST: Twin volcanoes in mist-wet ridges; basalt slopes to fringing reef shelves offshore
SV: Tight cluster of stratovolcanoes; ash plains on a narrow Pacific coastal strip
SY: Coastal mountains; rain-shadow interior steppe with uplifted fossil reef mesas inland
SZ: Basalt highveld grassland; fault scarp east to low bushveld and subtropical thicket
TD: Sand seas against volcanic massifs; braided channels on bleached interior clay pans
TG: Coastal lagoons by low fault ranges; northward savanna on rolling lateritic uplands
TH: Southern granite ranges; inland megafan braided by monsoon-charged river channels
